name: Broadcast
description: Jellyfin Broadcast generates a continuous programming schedule from your media library and exposes it as a live channel with M3U/XMLTV support.
overview: Turns your Jellyfin library into a single always-on TV channel

versions.changelog: V1 simplification: the config page no longer exposes Channel settings or a Programming Block editor -- ships with a single default 'All Day Movies' block (all libraries, no filters) that just works out of the box. Config page now shows only the Watch URLs (M3U/XMLTV/Stream) and a Regenerate button. A runtime fallback ensures any config that previously persisted an empty Blocks list still gets the default instead of staying permanently empty.

Fix: the TimeZone autocomplete field's initialization code ran at script top-level instead of inside the pageshow handler (unlike every other DOM-touching part of the config page). In real dashboard load timing this threw before the rest of the script -- Programming Blocks table, Add Block button, copy-to-clipboard buttons, and the Save Channel Settings form handler -- ever got registered, silently breaking the entire config page. Deferred into pageshow and wrapped defensively.

Fix: the plugin package didn't include a meta.json assembly whitelist, so Jellyfin's plugin loader fell back to loading every *.dll found anywhere under the plugin folder -- including bundled native SQLite binaries under runtimes/**/*.dll (e.g. runtimes/win-arm/native/e_sqlite3.dll), which are not managed assemblies. Loading any one of those throws BadImageFormatException and disables the entire plugin ('Malfunctioned'). The package now whitelists only the real managed assemblies via meta.json. 0.2.4.0 is broken -- do not install it, use this version instead.

BROKEN — do not install, use 0.2.4.1 instead (plugin loader disables this build with Malfunctioned status: no meta.json assembly whitelist, so it tries to load bundled native SQLite runtime binaries as managed assemblies). Fix: regenerating the schedule (daily task, library changes, config saves) advanced each block's Active Series and inflated movie airing counts by a full schedule's worth every time, and cut off whatever was currently playing. Active Series/Cooldown history is now committed from what actually aired, and the currently-playing program is preserved across regeneration. Fix: a full library query ran per scheduled program instead of once per block, and movie history was queried per candidate per pick instead of once per run -- both are now cached/batched. Fix: a DST spring-forward could crash regeneration; overlapping Programming Blocks are now rejected at validation; the XMLTV guide was missing whatever's airing right now. Security: manual Regenerate now requires elevated privileges; the config page no longer embeds the admin's session token in the M3U/XMLTV/Stream URLs. Config page: block fields are now shown/hidden based on content type and ordering.

Fix: plugin was disabled at startup because it referenced Jellyfin.Controller/Jellyfin.Model 10.11.11 while the target server runs 10.11.8 -- Jellyfin's plugin loader binds these by exact assembly version with no redirects. Now pinned to 10.11.8. Also fixed the earlier Windows-native e_sqlite3.dll packaging issue.

Fix: plugin was disabled at startup (Malfunctioned) because Jellyfin's plugin loader tries to load every *.dll under the plugin folder as a managed assembly, including the bundled Windows-native e_sqlite3.dll. That native binary is no longer packaged. Also: config page now shows ready-to-copy M3U/XMLTV/Stream URLs.

Fix: plugin failed to load ("NotSupported") because Microsoft.Data.Sqlite and its native SQLite dependencies weren't packaged alongside the plugin DLL. Also: config page now shows ready-to-copy M3U/XMLTV/Stream URLs (with API key included) for adding the channel to Jellyfin's own Live TV.
Config page now shows ready-to-copy M3U/XMLTV/Stream URLs (with API key included) for adding the channel to Jellyfin's own Live TV as a tuner + guide.
